items.q: Why is a food web a better model than a food chain?
items.correct: A food chain shows one linear path; a food web shows the many interconnected feeding relationships in reality, so it better represents how species depend on multiple food sources.
items.misconception: A food web is the same as a food chain, just drawn differently.
items.random_wrong: A food web shows what spiders eat and a food chain shows what other animals eat.
items.why: Most species have multiple predators and prey. A linear chain is a simplification — the web captures redundancy and resilience. Removing one species rarely collapses a real ecosystem.

items.q: Why are there no volcanoes at conservative plate boundaries?
items.correct: Conservative boundaries involve plates sliding past each other horizontally — no crust is created or destroyed, so there is no subduction to generate magma.
items.misconception: There are no volcanoes because the boundaries are too cold.
items.random_wrong: There are volcanoes at conservative boundaries — that's where most of them form.
items.why: Volcanism requires magma generation, which needs either subduction or mantle upwelling. Conservative boundaries (like the San Andreas) lack both, but still produce major earthquakes.

items.correct: The area of land drained by a river and its tributaries, bounded by a watershed (high ground that separates one basin from another).
items.misconception: A drainage basin is the bowl-shaped area at the mouth of the river.
items.random_wrong: A drainage basin is the underground source of a river.
items.why: Students take 'basin' too literally as a bowl. It is the entire catchment area — often a huge region, not a depression near the river itself.
Correct answer: Human Development Index. “High Development Indicator” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Norway. “UK” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many stages in Rostow's model?
items.why: The real figure is 5 stages. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
